message.content:
You are diagnosing a real bug for a careful refactor. Tools: Bash, Read, Grep. Work in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

QUESTION: How is the `search_more_evidence` tool invoked and COUNTED in the outline-agent loop? Read /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py and the tool-call plumbing. Determine: does the search counter observe ATTEMPTED vs SUCCESSFUL vs FAILED calls? Could the counter read 0 while calls actually happen (broken instrumentation), or is search genuinely never invoked? Quote the exact counter/increment code with file:line.

Actually READ the code (don't guess). Write your findings to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/search_diag/instrumentation.md (concrete, with file:line quotes + a clear conclusion). Reply ONLY: "instrumentation: <one-line conclusion>".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1720	    # -- execute ------------------------------------------------------------
1721	
1722	    async def _execute(self, decision: ReactDecision) -> AnalysisStep:
1723	        t0 = time.monotonic()
1724	        # Iter-3 P1 fix (real THINNED-corpus run: 8 real search_more_evidence fetches, 31
1725	        # genuinely new rows, ALL orphaned — the loop spent its ENTIRE retrieval budget
1726	        # re-chasing the SAME "Summary Table" section label under 8 differently-worded aspects,
1727	        # each individually terminated by the iter-3 P0 fix but never PREVENTED from being
1728	        # tried again). VETO before spending any real wall-clock/tokens on a fetch whose target
1729	        # section is already banked as structurally unhomeable this run — cheap, no network
1730	        # call, no LLM call.
1731	        veto_reason: Optional[str] = None
1732	        if decision.action == "search_more_evidence":
1733	            veto_sec = str((decision.action_input or {}).get("section", ""))
1734	            if (
1735	                _normalize_section_label(veto_sec) in self.workspace.unhomeable_sections
1736	                and self.workspace.required_titles
1737	                and self._resolve_section_title(veto_sec) is None
1738	            ):
1739	                veto_reason = (
1740	                    f"section {veto_sec!r} already proven unhomeable this run (no matching "
1741	                    f"required section title, required_titles="
1742	                    f"{self.workspace.required_titles!r}) — vetoed before any real fetch, "
1743	                    "regardless of aspect rewording"
1744	                )
1745	        tool_def = self.registry.get_tool(decision.action)
1746	        if veto_reason is not None:
1747	            result = ToolResult(
1748	                success=False, tool_name=decision.action,
1749	                markdown=f"search_more_evidence VETOED: {veto_reason}",
1750	                error="unhomeable_section_vetoed",
1751	            )
1752	            self.workspace.disclose(f"search_more_evidence VETOED: {veto_reason}")
1753	        elif not tool_def or not tool_def.execute:
1754	            result = ToolResult(
1755	                success=False, tool_name=decision.action,
1756	                markdown=f"Unknown tool: {decision.action}", error="unknown_tool",
1757	            )
1758	        else:
1759	            # W3 (2026-07-11): the dispatch used to hardcode ``client=None``, so execute_python
1760	            # failed 100% of the time with "No LLM client available" (tool_registry.py:556) — while
1761	            # still being advertised as available in the decide prompt. It now gets a real client on
1762	            # the CODE model, per-call, closed on every exit path (mirrors _decide, :1203).
1763	            # Gated on the tool that actually CONSUMES the client, not on requires_llm:
1764	            # search_more_evidence is also requires_llm=True but ignores the kwarg and builds its own
1765	            # clients internally (:1036), so gating on the flag would construct+close a wasted
1766	            # OpenRouterClient on every search turn.
1767	            code_client = None
1768	            try:
1769	                if decision.action in _CODEGEN_TOOLS:
1770	                    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1771	                        OpenRouterClient,
1772	                    )
1773	                    code_client = OpenRouterClient(model=outliner_code_model())
1774	                result = await tool_def.execute(
1775	                    evidence_store=self.workspace.ev_store,
1776	                    data_points=self.workspace.notebook.data_points,
1777	                    client=code_client,
1778	                    **(decision.action_input or {}),
1779	                )
1780	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single tool must never crash the loop
1781	                logger.warning("[outline_agent] tool %r raised: %s", decision.action, exc)
1782	                result = ToolResult(
1783	                    success=False, tool_name=decision.action,
1784	                    markdown=f"Tool raised: {exc}", error=str(exc)[:500],
1785	                )
1786	            finally:
1787	                if code_client is not None and hasattr(code_client, "close"):
1788	                    try:
1789	                        await code_client.close()
1790	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1791	                        pass
1792	        step = AnalysisStep(
1793	            step_number=self.workspace.turn, reasoning=decision.reasoning,
1794	            tool_name=decision.action, result=result,
1795	            elapsed_seconds=round(time.monotonic() - t0, 3),
1796	        )
1797	        self.workspace.notebook.add_step(step)
1798	        self._tool_failure_gap_check(step)
1799	        self._compute_result_check(step)
1800	
1801	        # Agent-initiated gap (detector 3): the decide step named a NEW section/aspect for
1802	        # search_more_evidence that was not already a ledger entry -> record it.
1803	        if decision.action == "search_more_evidence":
1804	            inp = decision.action_input or {}
1805	            sec, asp = str(inp.get("section", "")), str(inp.get("aspect", ""))
1806	            if asp and veto_reason is not None:
1807	                # Iter-3 P1 fix: a vetoed attempt must land STRAIGHT in UNFILLED, never PENDING
1808	                # — routing it through the generic add()+mark_in_progress()+retry-cap cycle
1809	                # would let it come back PENDING for up to `_gap_retries_per_aspect()` more
1810	                # rounds (cheap now that the real fetch is skipped, but still churns turns).
1811	                self.workspace.gap_ledger.add_unfillable(
1812	                    section=sec, aspect=asp, reason=veto_reason, source="agent",
1813	                )
1814	            elif asp:
1815	                existing = self.workspace.gap_ledger.get(sec, asp)
1816	                if existing is None:
1817	                    existing = self.workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1818	                        section=sec, aspect=asp, source="agent",
1819	                    )
1820	                self.workspace.gap_ledger.mark_in_progress(existing)
1821	                if not result.success:
1822	                    # A fetch that found nothing on-topic never gets to "did it cover the
1823	                    # aspect" — it plainly didn't fill anything.
1824	                    self.workspace.gap_ledger.mark_retry_or_unfilled(
1825	                        existing, f"search_more_evidence failed: {result.error}",
1826	                    )
1827	                else:
1828	                    # Iter-2 P0-2 fix: a successful fetch used to leave the new rows orphaned in
1829	                    # the pool — the outline never mutated, so the SAME gap kept re-firing every
1830	                    # round and the loop could not converge. AUTO-ASSIGN the surviving new ev_ids
1831	                    # to the section that triggered the search (a validated `reassign` op through
1832	                    # the SAME outline_revise apply path `update_outline` uses — never a free
1833	                    # rewrite) BEFORE the after-fold checklist re-runs, so the checklist judges
1834	                    # the outline as it will actually ship, not the stale pre-fold draft.
1835	                    new_ev_ids = list(result.source_evidence_ids or [])
1836	                    resolved_title = self._resolve_section_title(sec)
1837	                    unhomeable_agent_gap = False
1838	                    if resolved_title and new_ev_ids:
1839	                        assign_result = await _tool_update_outline(
1840	                            self.workspace,
1841	                            ops={"ops": [{
1842	                                "op": "reassign", "title": resolved_title,
1843	                                "add_ev_ids": new_ev_ids,
1844	                            }]},
1845	                        )
1846	                        if assign_result.success:
1847	                            self.workspace.disclose(
1848	                                f"auto-assign: routed {len(new_ev_ids)} new ev_id(s) to "
1849	                                f"section {resolved_title!r} (P0-2 fix — search fold-in now "

content.text: Now let me look at the final metrics block (around 2639) and the `new_evidence_count` computation, plus check how "search count" might be reported. Let me read the return/metrics region and the P2-1 fix comment.

content.content:
2600	    # ``asyncio.wait_for`` is belt-and-suspenders on top of the loop's own internal
2601	    # turns/wall-clock check (a single hung LLM call inside one turn must not be able to wedge
2602	    # the whole stage past the caller's own upstream timeout).
2603	    degraded_to_seed = False
2604	    degrade_reason = ""
2605	    try:
2606	        final_ws = await asyncio.wait_for(
2607	            agent.run(),
2608	            timeout=agent.wall_seconds + _env_int(
2609	                "PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS",
2610	                PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
2611	            ),
2612	        )
2613	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — degrade to SEED, never abort the outline stage
2614	        degraded_to_seed = True
2615	        degrade_reason = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"

content.text: This is the key finding. The "search counter" is `new_evidence_count` (line 2639), an indirect proxy computed from evidence-store size delta — not a direct count of search_more_evidence invocations. And there was a documented bug (P2-1 fix) where it read 0 due to aliasing. Let me verify the current state of the fix and check whether there's a more direct per-tool counter. Let me look at the loop body and how tools get counted per-turn, plus the disclosure counting.

content.content:
1980	    # -- main loop ------------------------------------------------------------
1981	
1982	    async def run(self) -> OutlineWorkspace:
1983	        ws = self.workspace
1984	        await self._run_checklist(trigger="seed")
1985	        ws.checkpoint(event="seed_checklist")
1986	
1987	        while ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds:
1988	            ws.turn += 1
1989	            try:
1990	                decision = await self._decide()
1991	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a decide failure ends the loop, not the run
1992	                logger.warning("[outline_agent] decide failed at turn %d: %s", ws.turn, exc)
1993	                ws.disclose(f"decide failed at turn {ws.turn}: {exc}; stopping loop")
1994	                break
1995	
1996	            if decision.action == _FINISH_ACTION:
1997	                deficiencies = await self._run_checklist(trigger=f"finish_attempt_turn_{ws.turn}")
1998	                pending = ws.gap_ledger.pending_count
1999	                budget_remains = ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds
2000	                if (deficiencies or pending) and budget_remains:
2001	                    ws.disclose(
2002	                        f"finish_outline BOUNCED at turn {ws.turn}: "
2003	                        f"{len(deficiencies)} new + {pending} pending gap(s) remain, "
2004	                        "budget available — continuing loop"
2005	                    )
2006	                    continue
2007	                ws.disclose(
2008	                    f"finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn {ws.turn}: "
2009	                    f"pending={pending}, budget_remains={budget_remains}"
2010	                )
2011	                break
2012	
2013	            await self._execute(decision)
2014	
2015	        # Any todo still PENDING at exit — whether it was retried to the cap or NEVER attempted
2016	        # (turns/wall ran out before the agent ever picked it up) — is honestly UNFILLED, not
2017	        # silently dropped. The two reasons are disclosed distinctly (§-1.3: every gap that
2018	        # wasn't filled is disclosed, never silently absent from the coverage/limitations trail).
2019	        for todo in ws.gap_ledger.all_todos:

content.text:
I have the full picture now. Key observations:

1. There is NO direct counter incremented on each `search_more_evidence` call. Every invocation goes through `_execute` (line 2013) and produces an `AnalysisStep` added to the notebook (line 1797). Steps carry `tool_name`, so search calls ARE recorded as steps.
2. The reported "search" telemetry is `new_evidence_count` (line 2639) — an evidence-store-size delta, which counts *net new rows kept*, NOT attempted/successful/failed calls.
3. `success` on the tool result is `n_kept > 0` (line 835) — so a call that fetches rows but keeps none reads as failed.
4. The documented P2-1 aliasing bug (lines 2442-2453) caused `new_evidence_count` to read 0 even when calls happened and rows were fetched, because `final_ws.ev_store is ev_store` (same object). Fixed by snapshotting `ev_store_size_before`/`ev_ids_before` before the loop.

Let me confirm whether steps can be counted by tool_name and check the P2-1 fix is actually in place (snapshot before loop). Let me verify the snapshot ordering relative to `agent.run()`.

input.description: Check ordering of snapshot vs agent construction/run
